ogTitle: North Stirlings vs North Tammin: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in North Stirlings and North Tammin.
heading: North Stirlings vs North Tammin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Stirlings, Western Australia and North Tammin, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Stirlings has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to North Tammin (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Stirlings or North Tammin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Stirlings has a flood risk score of 52/100, while North Tammin scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Stirlings scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. North Tammin scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Stirlings or North Tammin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Stirlings has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
